allow: PowerShell($env:PATH += ";$env:USERPROFILE\\.cargo\\bin"; cd "D:\\Vibe-coding\\Silent voice\\src-tauri"; cargo check 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 30)

Bash(agy --print "Research question for a Windows Rust app \(research only, no code changes\): I have a Grammarly-clone feature. A background thread uses UI Automation \(IUIAutomation, MTA COM\) to read the focused text field of ANY app, lint it, and draw squiggle overlays. Now when the user clicks a suggestion in my popup, I must REPLACE a character range \(start..end char indices\) inside the other app's focused edit control with the replacement word — without stealing focus, without clobbering the clipboard if avoidable, ideally preserving the app's undo stack. Compare these approaches and give a concrete recommendation with fallback order: \(1\) IUIAutomationTextRange::Select\(\) on the range then SendInput with KEYEVENTF_UNICODE to type the replacement; \(2\) ValuePattern::SetValue with the whole modified string; \(3\) Select\(\) then clipboard-swap + Ctrl+V paste + restore clipboard; \(4\) TextEditPattern or anything newer. For each: which apps/frameworks it works in \(classic Win32 EDIT/RichEdit, Notepad on Win11, Chromium/Electron textareas, WPF, WinUI\), known pitfalls \(caret position afterwards, IME, focus requirements — does Select\(\) require the window to be foreground? does SendInput go to the focused window only?\), and undo-stack behavior. Cite MSDN/UIA docs behavior where possible. Output a short decision matrix + recommended primary approach and fallback.")

Bash(agy --print "Research question \(research only, no code changes\): For a Grammarly-style suggestion popup implemented as a native Win32 WS_EX_NOACTIVATE topmost popup window on Windows 11: \(1\) After the user clicks a suggestion and the target app's text is programmatically edited, what does Grammarly \(and similar tools like LanguageTool desktop\) do about caret placement and scroll position? \(2\) When typing replacement text into another app via SendInput KEYEVENTF_UNICODE, are there timing/ordering pitfalls \(needing small delays between events, apps dropping fast synthetic input, needing to send both keydown and keyup per char\)? \(3\) Chromium/Electron specifics: does programmatic UIA TextRange::Select\(\) work on a contenteditable/textarea in Chrome/Edge, and does the page see the typed SendInput characters as real input events \(so React state updates\)? Short practical answer per point.")
